1. Engage your core and glutes to maintain stability and support your lower back throughout the exercise.
2. Perform the arm movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.
3. Focus on keeping your legs and chest lifted off the floor to maintain the Superman hold.

translations.name: Assisted chin up normal width reverse grip
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with a reverse grip (palms facing you) at shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: assisted 친업 normal width 리버스 grip
Assisted Chin-up on a bench
translations.instructions:
1. Stand on a bench with your hands gripping a pull-up bar, palms facing towards you (supinated grip).
2. Use your legs to assist in pulling your body up towards the bar until your chin is above the bar.
3. Lower your body back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
4. Continue the movement in a controlled, rhythmic manner, using the bench for assistance.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on keeping your body aligned and avoid swinging to ensure proper form.
2. Use your legs for assistance, but focus on using your arms and back muscles to perform the pull-up.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you pull up and inhaling as you lower yourself down.

translations.name: Assisted chin up reverse close grip
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with a reverse grip (palms facing you) positioned close together.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted chin up reverse wide grip
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with a reverse grip (palms facing you) positioned wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted close grip underhand chin up
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with a reverse grip (palms facing you) at shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted parallel close grip pull up
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with a parallel grip (palms facing each other) positioned at shoulder-width apart..
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted pull up
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with an overhand grip (palms facing forward) positioned just wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted pull up close grip
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) positioned close together.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted pull up normal grip
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with an overhand grip (palms facing forward) positioned just wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted pull up wide grip
translations.instructions:
1. Kneel on the platform pad of an assisted chin-up machine and grasp the handles with an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) positioned wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the movement by pulling your body upward until your chin is above the bar.
3. Slowly lower your body back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid flaring them out.
2. Use an appropriate level of assistance on the machine to ensure you can perform the exercise correctly.
3. Exhale as you pull your body upward and inhale as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Assisted sit ups
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your back on the floor, with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, close together. Have your partner hold your feet, holding them securely for stability.
2. Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head.
3. Engage your core and lift your upper body towards your knees.
4. Slowly lower your upper body back to the starting position with control. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you lift your upper body towards your knees and inhale as you lower it back down.
2. Perform the exercise slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and effectiveness.
3. Keep your elbows out to the sides and avoid pulling on your neck with your hands.

translations.name: Assisted weighted push up
translations.instructions:
1. Position yourself in a high plank position with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, your feet together, and your body forming a straight line from head to heels. Have your partner place a weight plate on your upper back.
2. Lower your body towards the floor by bending your elbows, until your chest nearly touches the floor.
3. Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position, maintaining a controlled and steady movement. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a straight line from head to heels to ensure proper form.
2. Exhale as you push up and inhale as you lower your body.
3. Ensure the weight plate is securely placed on your upper back for safety.

translations.name: Back and forward leg swings
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and use a nearby support (such as a squat cage, wall or a chair) for balance.
2. Swing your right leg forward and backward in a controlled, rhythmic manner.
3. After completing the desired number of swings with your right leg, switch to your left leg and repeat the movement.
4. Focus on smooth and controlled swings, avoiding excessive momentum.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on maintaining a steady rhythm to ensure controlled movements.
2. Ensure your upper body remains upright and avoid leaning forward or backward.
3. Keep your supporting leg slightly bent to enhance balance and reduce strain on your joints.

translations.name: back and shoulder stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on a block with your back straight, feet shoulder-width apart and raise your right arm overhead.
2. Bend your right elbow and reach down your back. Use your left hand to gently pull on your right elbow, enhancing the stretch.
3. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
4. Switch arms and repeat the stretch on the left side.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your core engaged to maintain balance and proper posture throughout the stretch.
2. Avoid pulling too hard on your elbow; the stretch should be gentle and not cause pain.
3. Breathe deeply and steadily to help relax your muscles and enhance the stretch.

translations.name: Back and Shoulders Stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on a block with your back straight, feet shoulder-width apart and raise your right arm overhead.
2. Bend your right elbow and reach down your back. Use your left hand to gently pull on your right elbow, enhancing the stretch.
3. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
4. Switch arms and repeat the stretch on the left side.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your core engaged to maintain balance and proper posture throughout the stretch.
2. Avoid pulling too hard on your elbow; the stretch should be gentle and not cause pain.
3. Breathe deeply and steadily to help relax your muscles and enhance the stretch.

translations.name: Back extension machine
translations.instructions:
1. Fix your feet to the footrests and press your thighs against the padded platform.
2. Place your arms across your chest and lean forward.
3. Raise yourself by leaning backward and forming an upright position.
4. Hold for a while, return to the initial position, and repeat.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your movements slow and controlled to maintain proper form and maximize muscle engagement.
2. Avoid hyperextending your back; stop when your body forms a straight line to prevent strain on your lower back.
3. Engage your core muscles throughout the exercise to support your spine and maintain stability.

translations.name: Back extension on exercise ball
translations.instructions:
1. Position your lower abdomen, face down on an exercise ball , with your feet planted firmly on the floor and your legs extended straight.
2. Place your hands lightly behind your head or cross them over your chest.
3. Bend at the waist to lower your upper body over the exercise ball, keeping your back straight.
4. Engage your lower back and glutes to lift your upper body back to the starting position, extending your back fully.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a stable position by keeping your feet firmly planted on the floor.
2. Avoid rounding your back; keep it straight to ensure proper form and prevent injury.
3. Focus on engaging your lower back muscles to lift your torso, not your neck or arms.

translations.name: Back Forward Leg Swings
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and use a nearby support (such as a squat cage, wall or a chair) for balance.
2. Swing your right leg forward and backward in a controlled, rhythmic manner.
3. After completing the desired number of swings with your right leg, switch to your left leg and repeat the movement.
4. Focus on smooth and controlled swings, avoiding excessive momentum.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on maintaining a steady rhythm to ensure controlled movements.
2. Ensure your upper body remains upright and avoid leaning forward or backward.
3. Keep your supporting leg slightly bent to enhance balance and reduce strain on your joints.

translations.name: Back lever
translations.instructions:
1. Start by hanging on a bar with your arms extended overhead, hands placed shoulder-width apart, and your palms facing forward.
2. Pull yourself up until your head is facing the floor and your legs are overhead.
3. Pass your legs under the bar and align your body in a straight line from head to toe and parallel to the floor.
4. Hold this position for a suitable duration.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on engaging your core and glutes to maintain a straight line from head to heels.
2. Ensure your grip on the bar is firm and secure to provide stability and prevent slipping.
3. Breathe steadily and evenly to help maintain the hold and reduce tension.

translations.name: Back pec stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and position yourself in front of an incline bench.
2. Lean forward and place your arms over the bench, with your palms facing down and your arms extended straight.
3. Allow your chest to lower towards the bench, feeling the stretch in your pectoral muscles and back.
4. Hold this position for 15-30 seconds, then release and return to the starting position.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your shoulders relaxed and down to avoid straining your neck.
2. Engage your core to maintain stability and support your lower back during the stretch.
3. Breathe deeply and steadily to help relax your muscles and enhance the stretch.

translations.name: Back slaps wrap around stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Swing your arms out to the sides, keeping them lightly bent.
3. Cross your arms in front of your body, wrapping them around yourself as if giving yourself a hug.
4. Continue the swinging motion, alternating which arm crosses on top with each repetition.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your movements fluid to promote relaxation and prevent stiffness.
2. Focus on extending your arms fully to each side to maximize the stretch in your chest and shoulders.
3. Maintain a steady pace to ensure you are not rushing through the movement and to allow a thorough stretch.

translations.name: Back Slaps Wrap Arround Stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Swing your arms out to the sides, keeping them lightly bent.
3. Cross your arms in front of your body, wrapping them around yourself as if giving yourself a hug.
4. Continue the swinging motion, alternating which arm crosses on top with each repetition.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your movements fluid to promote relaxation and prevent stiffness.
2. Focus on extending your arms fully to each side to maximize the stretch in your chest and shoulders.
3. Maintain a steady pace to ensure you are not rushing through the movement and to allow a thorough stretch.

translations.name: Back squeeze
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on a box with your feet flat on the floor and your arms extended straight out in front of you at shoulder height.
2. Pull your elbows back, squeezing your shoulder blades together and opening your chest.
3. Extend your arms back to the starting position in front of you.
4. Continue the movement in a controlled, rhythmic manner, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together with each repetition.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your core engaged to maintain stability and support your lower back throughout the exercise.
2. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together to maximize the activation of your upper back muscles.
3. Maintain an upright posture and avoid leaning forward or backward during the movement.

translations.name: Back stretch dynamic
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and position yourself in front of an incline bench.
2. Lean forward and place your hands on the incline bench, with your arms extended and your back straight parallel to the floor.
3. Push your hips back while keeping your hands on the bench, feeling the stretch in your back and hamstrings.
4. Repeat your hip motion, creating a dynamic stretch.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your back straight throughout the movement to ensure a proper stretch and prevent strain.
2. Focus on pushing your hips downwards rather than bending at the waist to enhance the stretch in your back.
3. Use controlled movements to avoid jerking or sudden motions that could lead to injury.

translations.name: Back stretch standing with bench
translations.instructions:
1. Stand tall near an incline bench with your back straight and feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Extend your arms and reach the bench by leaning forward.
3. Hold the bench and extend your back while lowering your chest towards the floor.
4. Hold this position for 15-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in your back.
translations.tips:
1. Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and support your lower back during the stretch.
2. Keep your shoulders relaxed and down away from your ears to prevent tension.
3. Make sure your feet are firmly planted on the floor to provide a stable base and enhance the stretch.

translations.name: Backhand raise
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees bent and lean forward at the hips until your torso is halfway parallel to the floor. Keep your back straight.
2. Extend your arms straight back parallel to your torso with your palms facing backwards.
3. Raise your arms upwards, keeping them straight, until they are over your back.
4. Lower your arms back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
translations.tips:
1. Engage your core to maintain stability and support your lower back throughout the exercise.
2. Keep your movements slow and controlled to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.
3. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement to enhance upper back activation.

translations.name: Backward abdominal stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Get down on your knees with your back straight, thighs extended, and legs resting on the floor.
2. Lean backward and hold your feet by extending your arms to stretch your abdominal muscles.
3. Maintain this stretch for 30-60 seconds.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your hips stable to ensure a deep and effective stretch.
2. Avoid overextending your back; only lean as far back as is comfortable and safe for your flexibility.
3. Breathe deeply and steadily to help relax your muscles and enhance the stretch.

translations.name: Backward Forward Turn to Side Neck Stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your head in a neutral position.
2. Tilt your head backward, stretching the front of your neck.
3. Return to the neutral position, then tilt your head forward to stretch the back of your neck.
4. Turn your head to one side, holding briefly, and then return to the neutral position. Repeat on the other side.
translations.tips:
1. Perform the movements slowly and gently to avoid straining your neck muscles.
2. Keep your shoulders relaxed and down to prevent tension in your upper back and shoulders.
3. Breathe deeply and steadily to help relax your muscles and enhance the stretch.

translations.name: 백워드 포워드 turn to 사이드 neck 스트레칭
Balance board lateral squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand on the balance board with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Shift your weight to one side and bend at the knees and hips to perform a squat, keeping your core engaged to maintain balance.
3. Return to the starting position and shift your weight to the other side, performing a squat on the opposite side.
4. Continue alternating sides in a controlled, rhythmic manner.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your knees aligned with your toes to prevent strain on your joints.
2. Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and support your lower back.
3. Focus on a point in front of you to help maintain balance and control.

translations.name: Balance Board (VERSION 2)
translations.instructions:
1. Stand on the balance board with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Shift your weight side to side by straightening one leg while lightly bending the other.
3. Shift your weight to the opposite side by bending the extended leg lightly and straightening the other leg.
4. Continue the movement in a controlled, rhythmic manner to improve balance.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on a point in front of you to help maintain your balance.
2. Start with slow movements and gradually increase the speed as you become more comfortable.
3. Keep your movements controlled to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.

translations.name: Ball Sit-up
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the floor and knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
2. Lean back, rolling the ball under your lower back, and place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
3. Engage your core and lift your upper body upwards until vertical to the floor.
4. Lower your upper body back down to the starting position, repeat.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maintain stability and proper form.
2. Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands to prevent strain; use your core muscles to lift your body.
3. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.

translations.name: Balloon Drill
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a balloon in front of you.
2. Toss the balloon into the air and tap it lightly to keep it afloat.
3. As the balloon is in the air, perform a squat by bending your knees and lowering your hips until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
4. Stand back up and tap the balloon again as it comes down, repeating the process.
translations.tips:
1. Start with a shallow squat if you are new to the exercise and gradually increase the depth as you become more comfortable.
2. Focus on keeping your back straight and your knees aligned with your toes during the squat.
3. Perform the movement smoothly and with control to maximize muscle activation and coordination.

translations.name: Band Assisted Chin Up (From Knee)
translations.instructions:
1. Loop a resistance band around a pull-up bar and secure the other end under one knee while keeping the other leg straight.
2. Grip the pull-up bar with an underhand grip (palms facing back), hands shoulder-width apart.
3. Using the assistance of the band, pull yourself up until your chin is above the bar.
4. Lower yourself back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely in place under your knee to provide consistent assistance and avoid injury.
2. Avoid using momentum; focus on using your arm and back muscles to perform the pull-up.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you pull up and inhaling as you lower yourself down.

translations.name: Band bent-over row
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the resistance band under both feet, ensuring it is secure. Hold one handle of the band in each hand with your palms facing each other, bending your torso forward at the hips while keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
2. Let your arms hang straight down toward the floor, fully extended, with your elbows slightly bent.
3. Pull the handles towards your torso by bending your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
4. Slowly lower the handles back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely placed under your feet to avoid slipping.
2. Exhale as you pull the band towards your torso and inhale as you lower it back down.
3. Perform the exercise slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement.

translations.name: Band bicycle crunch
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your back on a mat and secure a resistance band around both feet. Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outward, and keep your legs extended straight.
2. Engage your core and lift your upper body off the floor. Bend your right knee towards your chest while simultaneously twisting your torso to bring your left elbow towards your right knee.
3. Switch sides by bending your left knee towards your chest while bringing your right elbow towards your left knee, performing a pedaling motion.
4. Continue alternating sides in a controlled manner for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands; use your abs to lift and twist your torso.
2. Ensure the resistance band is securely placed around your feet and on the equipment to avoid slipping.
3. Maintain a steady rhythm and focus on controlled movements rather than speed.

translations.name: Band Chest Fly
translations.instructions:
1. Secure the middle of the resistance band to a sturdy anchor point at head height. Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding one handle of the band in each hand. 
2. Extend your arms out to the sides with a slight bend in your elbows, keeping your palms facing forward. Step forward to create tension in the band, positioning your hands slightly below shoulder height.
3. Move your arms in a wide arc motion, bringing your hands together in front of your chest. Squeeze your chest muscles as your hands meet, keeping a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
4. Slowly return your arms to the starting position with control, maintaining tension in the band. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely anchored to avoid slipping.
2. Exhale as you bring your hands together and inhale as you return to the starting position.
3. Focus on squeezing your chest muscles as you bring your hands together.

translations.name: Band close-grip biceps curl
translations.instructions:
1. Stand tall with your back straight, feet shoulder-width apart, and a Resistance band under your feet.
2. Hold the ends of the bands in your hands while keeping your arms straight and palms facing forward.
3. Raise the band by folding your arms to a 90° angle while taking your hands close to each other.
4. Slowly lower the handles back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely placed under your feet to avoid slipping.
2. Exhale as you curl the band towards your shoulders and inhale as you lower it back down.
3. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid swinging them forward or backward.

translations.name: Band Close Grip Row
translations.instructions:
1. Secure the middle of the resistance band to a sturdy anchor point at chest height. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding one handle of the band in each hand with your palms facing each other and kept close together. Extend your arms in front of your chest, ensuring the band is taut.
2. Pull the handles towards your chest until your hands are close to your chest, bending your elbows and keeping them pointing outward.
3. Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position with control, maintaining tension in the band. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely anchored to avoid slipping.
2. Exhale as you pull the handles towards your chest and inhale as you return to the starting position.
3. Use a resistance band with appropriate tension to ensure you can perform the exercise with good form.

translations.name: Band Concentration Curl
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, placing the middle of the resistance band under your right foot. Hold the handle of the band in your right hand, and rest your elbow against the inside of your thigh, fully extending your arm towards the floor.
2. Curl the handle upward towards your shoulder, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement until your hand is near shoulder level.
3. Slowly lower the handle back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arm. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the left arm.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely placed under your foot to avoid slipping.
2. Exhale as you curl the band upwards and inhale as you lower it back down.
3. Keep your upper arm stationary and avoid swinging it.

translations.name: Band cross abduction
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place the resistance band under both feet, crossing the band in front of you. Hold the handles of the band in opposite hands with arms hanging in front of your hips.
2. Keeping your right leg straight, lift it sideways away from your body until your leg is fully extended to the side.
3. Slowly lower your leg back to the starting position with control, keeping tension in the band.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the left leg.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely placed under your feet and properly crossed to avoid slipping.
2. Keep your upper body stationary and avoid leaning to the side.
3. Perform the exercise slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement.

translations.name: Band deadlift
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place the resistance band under both feet, holding one handle of the band in each hand with your arms extended straight down, palms facing your hips.
2. Hinge at your hips and bend your knees, keeping your back straight, lowering your torso until it is almost parallel to the floor.
3. Press through your heels and extend your hips and knees to stand back up straight, bringing the handles up along your legs until you are fully upright.
4. Repeat the exercise for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely placed under your feet to avoid slipping.
2. Exhale as you stand up and inhale as you lower back down.
3. Use a resistance band with appropriate tension to ensure you can perform the exercise with good form.

translations.name: Band Incline T Raise
translations.instructions:
1. Lie chest-first on an incline bench set at a 45-degree angle, holding one handle of the resistance band in each hand with your arms fully extended down, palms facing forward. Keep your feet firm on the floor and the resistance band secured under the bench.
2. Lift your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, forming a "T" shape with your body.
3. Pause briefly at the top of the movement, ensuring your arms are parallel and your torso is stable against the bench.
4. Lower your arms back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you lift your arms and inhale as you lower them back down.
2. Perform the exercise slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement.
3. Keep your neck in a neutral position by looking at the bench, not forward or down.

translations.name: Band Incline Triceps Extension
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on an incline bench set at a 45-degree angle. Place the resistance band under the bench and hold one handle of the band in each hand with your arms extended straight above your head, palms facing forward.
2. Lower the handles behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
3. Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement, ensuring your forearms are parallel to the floor and your upper arms remain still.
4. Extend your arms back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure the resistance band is securely anchored under the bench to avoid slipping.
2. Maintain a neutral wrist position to reduce strain on your joints.
3 .Focus on squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.

translations.name: Band Lying Reverse Grip Pressdown
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a resistance band anchored behind your head. Hold the ends of the band with your palms facing forward with your elbows bent and forearms parallel to the floor.
2. Extend your arms straight up to the ceiling, keeping your elbows stationary.
3. Slowly return your hands to the starting position, controlling the resistance.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Use a controlled motion to extend and return your arms, preventing any jerking movements.
2. Use an appropriate resistance band that challenges you without compromising your form.
3. Perform the exercise slowly and with control to increase muscle activation and reduce the risk of injury.

translations.name: Barbell Back Wide Shrug
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands behind your back. Your palms should face away from your body with a wide grip.
2. Keep your shoulders back and chest up. Slowly lift your shoulders towards your ears in a shrugging motion.
3. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement, holding the contraction for a moment.
4. Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Avoid rotating your shoulders; lift them straight up and down to prevent strain.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your shoulders and inhaling as you lower them.
3. Keep your arms straight throughout the exercise to focus the movement on your shoulders.

translations.name: Barbell back wide shrugs
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands behind your back. Your palms should face away from your body with a wide grip.
2. Keep your shoulders back and chest up. Slowly lift your shoulders towards your ears in a shrugging motion.
3. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement, holding the contraction for a moment.
4. Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Avoid rotating your shoulders; lift them straight up and down to prevent strain.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your shoulders and inhaling as you lower them.
3. Keep your arms straight throughout the exercise to focus the movement on your shoulders.

translations.name: Barbell behind neck push press
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands behind your neck. Your hands should be slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, with the barbell resting on your upper back and shoulders.
2. Slightly bend your knees, preparing to use your legs to generate power.
3. Push through your heels and extend your knees and hips explosively, pressing the barbell overhead until your arms are fully extended. Keep the bar slightly behind your head at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position behind your neck with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Avoid excessive backward lean by engaging your core and keeping your chest up.
2. Exhale forcefully as you press the barbell overhead and inhale as you bring it back down.
3. Ensure a smooth and controlled descent to maintain proper form and prevent injury.

translations.name: Barbell Behind the Back Push Press Single Arm Balance
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with one hand behind your back. Your hand should be slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, with the barbell resting on your upper back and shoulder.
2. Slightly bend your knees, preparing to use your legs to generate power.
3. Push through your heels and extend your knees and hips explosively, pressing the barbell overhead with one arm until your arm is fully extended. Keep the bar slightly behind your head at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position behind your shoulder with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain balance by focusing on a fixed point in front of you and keeping your core engaged.
2. Exhale forcefully as you press the barbell overhead and inhale as you bring it back down.
3. Ensure a smooth and controlled descent to maintain proper form and prevent injury.

translations.name: 바벨 behind the back push 프레스 싱글 암 balance
Barbell Bench Lateral Step-up
translations.instructions:
1. Stand beside a bench with a barbell across your upper back. Your hands should grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Step laterally onto the bench with your closest foot, pushing through your heel to lift your body up onto the bench.
3. Bring your trailing leg up to meet the stepping leg by the bench, maintaining balance and control.
4. Step down off the bench with your trailing leg first, then the stepping leg. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your entire foot is placed on the bench to provide a stable base for the step-up.
2. Move slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you step up and inhaling as you step down.

translations.name: Barbell bench press
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor. Grip the barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, and position the bar directly above your chest.
2. Engage your core and unrack the barbell by extending your arms fully. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle to your body.
3. Press the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms fully, focusing on using your chest muscles.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully rack the barbell back onto the bench press rack.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your feet flat on the floor to maintain stability and support your lower back.
2. Engage your core to maintain a stable and strong torso throughout the movement.
3. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to prevent injury and maximize muscle engagement.

translations.name: Barbell bench press decline
translations.instructions:
1. Set a decline bench at a suitable angle and lie down on it with your feet secured under the foot pads. Grip the barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Engage your core and unrack the barbell by extending your arms fully. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to your lower chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle to your body.
3. Press the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms fully, focusing on using your chest muscles.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully rack the barbell back onto the bench press rack.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your feet secured under the foot pads to maintain stability and prevent sliding.
2. Engage your core to maintain a stable and strong torso throughout the movement.
3. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to prevent injury and maximize muscle engagement.

translations.name: Barbell bench press incline
translations.instructions:
1. Set an incline bench to a 45 degree angle and lie down on it with your feet flat on the floor. Grip the barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Engage your core and unrack the barbell by extending your arms fully. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to your upper chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your body.
3. Press the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms fully, focusing on using your chest muscles.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully rack the barbell back onto the bench press rack.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your feet flat on the floor to maintain stability and support your lower back.
2. Engage your core to maintain a stable and strong torso throughout the movement.
3. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to prevent injury and maximize muscle engagement.

translations.name: Barbell Bench Squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, back facing a bench, holding a barbell across your upper back. Your hands should grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Keep your chest up. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until you sit down on the bench.
3. Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your knees track over your toes to prevent knee injuries and ensure proper form.
2. Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement to maintain proper alignment.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell bench squats
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, back facing a bench, holding a barbell across your upper back. Your hands should grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Keep your chest up. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until you sit down on the bench.
3. Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your knees track over your toes to prevent knee injuries and ensure proper form.
2. Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement to maintain proper alignment.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell bent over row pronated grip
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands in a pronated grip (palms facing backward), and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Slightly bend your knees, and hinge at your hips to lower your torso until it is almost parallel to the floor. Keep your back straight and your gaze forward.
3. Pull the barbell towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement. Keeping your elbows close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, extending your arms fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Avoid rounding your back by keeping your chest up and your spine neutral.
2. Move the barbell slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you pull the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it back down.

translations.name: 바벨 bent over 로우 pronated grip
Barbell bent over row supinated grip
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands in a supinated grip (palms facing forward), and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Slightly bend your knees, and hinge at your hips to lower your torso until it is almost parallel to the floor. Keep your back straight and your gaze forward.
3. Pull the barbell towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement. Keeping your elbows close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, extending your arms fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Avoid rounding your back by keeping your chest up and your spine neutral.
2. Move the barbell slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you pull the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it back down.

translations.name: 바벨 bent over 로우 supinated grip
Barbell bicep curl close grip
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands using a close supinated grip (palms facing foreward). Your hands should be closer than shoulder-width apart.
2. Engage your core and keep your elbows close to your torso. Slowly curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, maintaining the supinated grip with your palms facing upward.
3. Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, ensuring your elbows stay stationary and close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, extending your arms fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
2. Keep your back straight and shoulders back to maintain proper posture.
3. Visualize the bicep contraction to enhance the mind-muscle connection for better results.

translations.name: Barbell bicep curl normal grip
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands using a supinated grip (palms facing forward). Your hands should be about shoulder-width apart.
2. Keep your elbows close to your torso. Slowly curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, maintaining the supinated grip with your palms facing upward.
3. Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, ensuring your elbows stay stationary and close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, extending your arms fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
2. Keep your back straight and shoulders back to maintain proper posture.
3. Visualize the bicep contraction to enhance the mind-muscle connection for better results.

translations.name: Barbell bicep curl wide grip
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands using a supinated grip (palms facing forward). Your hands should be about a fist wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Keep your elbows close to your torso. Slowly curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, maintaining the supinated grip with your palms facing upward.
3. Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, ensuring your elbows stay stationary and close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, extending your arms fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
2. Keep your back straight and shoulders back to maintain proper posture.
3. Visualize the bicep contraction to enhance the mind-muscle connection for better results.

translations.name: Barbell biceps curl
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands using a supinated grip (palms facing forward). Your hands should be about shoulder-width apart.
2. Keep your elbows close to your torso. Slowly curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, maintaining the supinated grip with your palms facing upward.
3. Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, ensuring your elbows stay stationary and close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, extending your arms fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
2. Keep your back straight and shoulders back to maintain proper posture.
3. Visualize the bicep contraction to enhance the mind-muscle connection for better results.

translations.name: Barbell box squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, back facing a sturdy box. Position a barbell across your upper back, gripping it with both hands, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Slowly lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your back straight.
3. Lower yourself until your glutes lightly touch the box or bench. Ensure your knees track over your toes and do not cave inward.
4. Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your knees track over your toes to prevent knee injuries and ensure proper form.
2. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell bulgarian split squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your back turned to a bench, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. 
2. Place one foot behind you on the bench, ensuring the top of your foot is resting on the bench.
3. Keep your chest up, and maintain a neutral spine. Begin the squat by bending your front knee and lowering your body until your back knee nearly touches the floor.
4. Push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position, extending your hip and knee fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your front knee tracks over your toes to prevent knee injuries and ensure proper form.
2. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell bulgarian split squat left front-back view
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your back turned to a box, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. 
2. Place one foot behind you on the box, ensuring the top of your foot is resting on the box.
3. Keep your chest up, and maintain a neutral spine. Begin the squat by bending your front knee and lowering your body until your back knee nearly touches the floor.
4. Push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position, extending your hip and knee fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your front knee tracks over your toes to prevent knee injuries and ensure proper form.
2. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: 바벨 bulgarian split 스쿼트 left front-back view
Barbell Bulgarian split squat left side view
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your back turned to a box, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. 
2. Place one foot behind you on the box, ensuring the top of your foot is resting on the box.
3. Keep your chest up, and maintain a neutral spine. Begin the squat by bending your front knee and lowering your body until your back knee nearly touches the floor.
4. Push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position, extending your hip and knee fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your front knee tracks over your toes to prevent knee injuries and ensure proper form.
2. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: 바벨 bulgarian split 스쿼트 left 사이드 view
Barbell clean and jerk split squat
translations.instructions:
1. Lean with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip. Clean the barbell to your shoulders by explosively lifting it from the floor, shrugging your shoulders, and rotating your wrists so the bar rests on your shoulders with your elbows pointing forward.
2. Engage your core and slightly dip your knees. Explosively push the barbell overhead by extending your legs and arms fully.
3. Step into a split squat position by moving one leg forward and the other leg back, lowering your body until your back knee nearly touches the floor. Ensure your front knee tracks over your toes and your back knee is bent.
4. Push through the heel of your front foot to stand back up, bringing your feet together while keeping the barbell overhead. Carefully lower the barbell back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating the leg that steps forward for each rep.
translations.tips:
1. Move explosively but with control to ensure proper form and maximize muscle engagement.
2. Ensure your knees track over your toes to prevent knee injuries and maintain proper alignment.
3. Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement to maintain proper posture.

translations.name: 바벨 clean and jerk split 스쿼트
Barbell clean and press
translations.instructions:
1. Start in a squatted position with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip. The barbell should be on the floor in front of you.
2. Explosively lift the barbell by extending your hips and knees, shrugging your shoulders, and rotating your wrists so the bar rests on your shoulders with your elbows pointing forward (clean position).
3. From the clean position, engage your core and press the barbell overhead by extending your arms fully. Keep the bar slightly behind your head at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to your shoulders with control, then return the barbell to the floor by reversing the clean movement. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on leg drive during the clean to generate power from your lower body.
2. Keep your grip firm but not too tight to allow smooth wrist rotation during the clean.
3. Exhale powerfully as you press the bar overhead to stabilize your core.

translations.name: Barbell clean deadlift
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out, with a barbell on the floor in front of you. Bend at your hips and knees to grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Engage your core, keep your back straight, and your chest up. Begin the lift by pushing through your heels, extending your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor.
3. As the barbell passes your knees, explosively extend your hips and knees while shrugging your shoulders to bring the barbell to your upper thighs.
4. Reverse the movement by lowering the barbell back to the floor with control, maintaining a straight back and engaged core. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a neutral spine to avoid rounding your back and prevent injury.
2. Use your legs and hips to drive the barbell up, minimizing the strain on your lower back.
3. Keep the bar close to your body throughout the lift to maintain proper form.

translations.name: Barbell clean grip front squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with a clean grip (palms facing up, elbows pointing forward). The barbell should rest on the front of your shoulders, and your fingers should be under the bar to support it.
2. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until your thighs are parallel to the floor or slightly below.
3. Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your elbows stay high throughout the movement to keep the barbell in place.
2. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell clean pull
translations.instructions:
1. Start in a squatted position with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out, holding a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. The barbell should be on the floor in front of you.
2. Begin the lift by pushing through your heels, extending your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor.
3. As the barbell passes your knees, explosively extend your hips and knees while shrugging your shoulders and pulling the barbell to your midsection and up to chest level.
4. Lower the barbell back to the floor with control, maintaining a straight back and engaged core. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on leg drive to generate power from your lower body during the lift.
2. Maintain a neutral spine to avoid rounding your back and prevent injury.
3. Use your legs and hips to drive the barbell up, minimizing the strain on your lower back.

translations.name: Barbell close grip bench press
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor. Grip the barbell with your hands placed closer than shoulder-width apart.
2. Unrack the barbell by extending your arms fully. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to your lower chest, keeping your elbows facing away from your body.
3. Press the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms fully, focusing on using your triceps and chest muscles.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully rack the barbell back onto the bench press rack.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
2. Keep your feet flat on the floor to maintain stability and support your lower back.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you press the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it back down.

translations.name: Barbell close grip press
translations.instructions:
1.Lie on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a barbell with your hands placed closer than shoulder-width apart, arms straight over your chest.
2. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to your lower chest, keeping your elbows tucked close to your body.
3. Press the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms fully, focusing on using your triceps muscles.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows tucked in close to your body to engage your triceps effectively.
2. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
3. Keep your feet flat on the floor to maintain stability and support your lower back.

translations.name: Barbell curtsy lunges
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Step your right leg behind and across your left leg, lowering your hips until your left thigh is parallel to the floor, and your right knee nearly touches the floor in a curtsy position.
3. Push through your front heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the other side by stepping your left leg behind and across your right leg. Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your front knee tracks over your toes to prevent knee injuries and ensure proper form.
2. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement to maintain proper alignment.

translations.name: Barbell deadlift
translations.instructions:
1. Bend at your hips and knees to grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the lift by pushing through your heels, extending your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor.
3. As the barbell passes your knees, continue to lift by extending your hips until you are standing upright with the barbell in front of your thighs.
4. Lower the barbell back to the floor with control by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, maintaining a straight back and engaged core. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your head in line with your spine to maintain proper neck alignment and prevent strain.
2. Visualize pulling the barbell back rather than up, to keep it close to your body and use the correct muscle groups.
3. Use a mixed grip (one hand overhand, one hand underhand) for heavier lifts to enhance grip strength.

translations.name: Barbell deadlift 360 degrees
translations.instructions:
1. Bend at your hips and knees to grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the lift by pushing through your heels, extending your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor.
3. As the barbell passes your knees, continue to lift by extending your hips until you are standing upright with the barbell in front of your thighs.
4. Lower the barbell back to the floor with control by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, maintaining a straight back and engaged core. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your head in line with your spine to maintain proper neck alignment and prevent strain.
2. Visualize pulling the barbell back rather than up, to keep it close to your body and use the correct muscle groups.
3. Use a mixed grip (one hand overhand, one hand underhand) for heavier lifts to enhance grip strength.

translations.name: Barbell Deadlift High Pull
translations.instructions:
1. Place a barbell on the floor in front of you. Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your toes pointed slightly outward. 
2. Hinge at your hips and bend your knees to lower your body, grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
3. Push through your heels to lift the barbell off the floor, extending your hips and knees simultaneously. As you reach the standing position, pull the barbell upward towards your chin by bending your elbows and keeping them pointed outward.
4. Lower the barbell back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Use a weight that allows you to maintain proper form throughout the exercise.
2. Push through your heels to maximize power and stability.
3. Ensure your knees track over your toes to prevent knee strain.

translations.name: Barbell drag biceps curl
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands using a supinated grip (palms facing up). Your hands should be about shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin by pulling your elbows back and curling the barbell up along your body, dragging it against your torso.
3. Continue to pull the barbell upward until it reaches chest level, keeping your elbows back and close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position, maintaining control and keeping the barbell close to your torso. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows back to ensure the barbell stays close to your torso throughout the movement.
2. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
3. Keep your back straight and shoulders back to maintain proper posture.

translations.name: Barbell drag curl
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with both hands using a supinated grip (palms facing up). Your hands should be about shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin by pulling your elbows back and curling the barbell up along your body, dragging it against your torso.
3. Continue to pull the barbell upward until it reaches chest level, keeping your elbows back and close to your body.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position, maintaining control and keeping the barbell close to your torso. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows back to ensure the barbell stays close to your torso throughout the movement.
2. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
3. Keep your back straight and shoulders back to maintain proper posture.

translations.name: Barbell floor chest press
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your back on the floor with your feet flat on the floor. Grip a barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, and hold the barbell directly above your chest with your arms bent, elbows on the floor.
2. Engage your core and slowly press the barbell up until your arms are straight over your chest.
3. Lower the barbell back to the starting position by bending your arms fully, focusing on using your chest muscles.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Lower the barbell slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
2. Ensure your elbows are at a 45-degree angle to your body to reduce shoulder strain.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you press the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it down.

translations.name: Barbell front chest bench squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, back facing a bench, holding a barbell across your upper chest. Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart, and keep your elbows pointing forward.
2. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until you sit down on the bench.
3. Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows high to maintain the barbell's position on your chest.
2. Engage your core to support your lower back and maintain proper posture.
3. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.

translations.name: Barbell front chest lunges
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper chest. Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart, and keep your elbows pointing forward.
2. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your body until your right thigh is parallel to the floor and your left knee nearly touches the floor.
3. Push through your front heel to return to the starting position. Repeat the movement with the same leg.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows high to maintain the barbell's position on your chest.
2. Engage your core to support your lower back and maintain proper posture.
3. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.

translations.name: Barbell front chest squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper chest. Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart, and keep your elbows pointing forward.
2. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until your thighs are parallel to the floor or slightly below.
3. Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.
3. Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement to maintain proper alignment.

translations.name: Barbell front lunges with small pause
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart, and keep your elbows pointing forward.
2. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your body until your right thigh is parallel to the floor and your left knee nearly touches the floor. Pause briefly in this position.
3. Push through your front heel to return to the starting position. Repeat the movement with the same leg.
translations.tips:
1. Pause for a second at the bottom of each lunge to enhance muscle activation and stability.
2. Drive through the heel of your front foot to engage your glutes and quadriceps more effectively.
3. Keep your head up and eyes forward to maintain balance and proper form.

translations.name: 바벨 front 런지s with small pause
Barbell front raise
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip in front of your thighs. Your hands should be shoulder-width apart.
2. Lift the barbell in front of you with straight arms until it reaches shoulder height.
3. Lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, keeping your arms straight. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a slight bend in your elbows to reduce strain on the joints.
2. Lift with your shoulders rather than swinging your body to ensure proper muscle engagement.
3. Keep your core tight to support your lower back and maintain proper posture.

translations.name: Barbell front squats
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper chest. Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart, and keep your elbows pointing forward.
2. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until your thighs are parallel to the floor or slightly below.
3. Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Lower your body slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.
3. Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement to maintain proper alignment.

translations.name: Barbell full clean
translations.instructions:
1. Bend at your hips and knees to grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the lift by pushing through your heels, extending your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor.
3. As the barbell passes your knees, explosively extend your hips and knees, lightly jumping, while shrugging your shoulders and pulling the barbell up to your chest level. Rotate your elbows under the bar to catch it in a front squat position.
4. Lower your body into a full squat with the barbell resting on your front shoulders, elbows high, and chest up. Push through your heels to stand back up, fully extending your hips and knees. Lower the barbell back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your elbows high during the catch to maintain the barbell's position on your shoulders.
2. Engage your core to support your lower back and maintain stability throughout the movement.
3. Use your legs and hips to generate power and drive the barbell up.

translations.name: Barbell full squat
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until your thighs are slightly below than parallel to the floor.
3. Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your chest up and back straight to maintain proper posture and alignment.
2. Focus on driving through your heels to engage your glutes and quadriceps effectively.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you stand up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell full squat back POV
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until your thighs are slightly below than parallel to the floor.
3. Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your chest up and back straight to maintain proper posture and alignment.
2. Focus on driving through your heels to engage your glutes and quadriceps effectively.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you stand up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell full squat side POV
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until your thighs are slightly below than parallel to the floor.
3. Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your chest up and back straight to maintain proper posture and alignment.
2. Focus on driving through your heels to engage your glutes and quadriceps effectively.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you stand up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Barbell full zercher squat
translations.instructions:
1. Position a barbell in a squat rack at about waist height. Stand close to the barbell and hook your arms under it, holding the bar in the crook of your elbows with your hands close in front of your chest.
2. Lift the barbell off the rack by standing up straight. Step back and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out.
3. Maintain a neutral spine as you begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering your body until your thighs are parallel to the floor or slightly below.
4. Push through your heels to return to the starting position, extending your hips and knees until you are fully upright. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Move slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.
3. Distribute your weight evenly across your feet to ensure balance and stability.

translations.name: Barbell glute bridge
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, shoulder-width apart. Place a barbell across your hips, holding it securely with both hands.
2. Push through your heels to lift your hips off the floor until your body forms a straight line from your shoulders to your knees.
3. Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement and hold for a brief moment.
4. Lower your hips back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Drive through your heels to engage your glutes and hamstrings effectively.
2. Ensure your knees stay in line with your toes and do not flare out or cave inward.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your hips and inhaling as you lower them back down.
4. Use a pad or towel on the barbell for added comfort on your hips.

translations.name: Barbell good morning
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Slightly bend your knees and hinge at your hips to lower your torso forward. Keep your back straight and your head in line with your spine.
3. Continue to lower your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings and glutes.
4. Engage your glutes and hamstrings to raise your torso back to the starting position, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement to prevent lower back strain.
2. Engage your core to support your lower back and maintain stability.
3. Hinge at your hips rather than bending your back to ensure proper form.

translations.name: Barbell hip thrust
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a bench, knees bent, and feet flat on the floor. Roll a barbell over your hips, holding it securely with both hands.
2. Push through your heels to lift your hips off the floor until your body forms a straight line from your shoulders to your knees.
3. Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement and hold for a brief moment, ensuring your hips are fully extended.
4. Lower your hips back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Drive through your heels to engage your glutes and hamstrings effectively.
2. Ensure your knees stay in line with your toes and do not flare out or cave inward.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your hips and inhaling as you lower them back down.
