2. Lift both legs off the bench a few inches, keeping them straight.
3. Begin the flutter kicks by alternately raising and lowering your legs in a scissor-like motion, moving your right leg up while your left leg moves down and vice versa.
4. Continue the flutter kicks for the desired number of repetitions or duration, keeping your core engaged and your movements controlled.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders to maintain proper form.
2. Perform the exercise on a stable, non-slip bench to ensure safety and prevent slipping.
3. If you are new to this exercise, start with a smaller range of motion and gradually increase it as you build strength.

translations.name: Seated hip abductor machine
translations.instructions:
1. Sit down on the hip abduction machine and place your feet flat on the footrests while positioning your outer thighs against the padded levers.
2. Grip the side handles to stabilize your upper body and press your legs outward against the resistance, pushing the pads apart in a controlled motion.
3. Pause for a second at the peak of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
4. Repeat for the desired repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Sit upright with your back firmly against the backrest to maintain proper posture.
2. Focus on squeezing your glutes and outer thighs throughout the movement.
3. Perform the movement slowly and avoid using momentum to push the legs outward.

translations.name: Seated leg curl machine
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your legs fully extended. Adjust the backrest and leg pads so the pad rests just above your heels.
2. Pull your heels down by bending your knees until the pad reaches down as far as possible.
3. Pause for a second, then slowly return to the starting position. 
4. Repeat for the desired repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you curl and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Do not let the weight stack drop; control the descent movement for maximum muscle activation.
3. Keep your toes pointed forward throughout the exercise.

translations.name: Seated Resistance band - cable rows
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the floor with your legs fully extended and feet together, anchoring the resistance band around the soles of your feet. Hold the handles with both hands, arms extended forward, palms facing each other.
2. Keep your back straight, chest up, and core engaged to stabilize your body.
3. Pull the handles towards your torso by bending your elbows, keeping your arms close to your body. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together at the peak of the movement.
4. Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position, maintaining control over the resistance band. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your back straight and avoid slouching to maintain proper form.
2. Engage your core to prevent any rocking or unnecessary movement during the exercise.
3. Use a controlled motion to pull and release the resistance band, avoiding jerking movements.

translations.name: Seated shoulder flexor depressor retractor stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you and your back straight.
2. Extend both arms down to your sides, then lean back slightly.
3. Bring both arms about a foot behind you, keeping them straight, and place your hands on the floor with your fingers pointing forward.
4. Hold this position for 15-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in your shoulders. Slowly return to the starting position.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your arms remain straight and your hands are firmly placed on the floor to maximize the stretch in your shoulders.
2. Avoid lifting your shoulders towards your ears; keep them relaxed and down to deepen the stretch.
3. Perform the stretch on a stable, non-slip surface to ensure safety and prevent slipping.

translations.name: 시티드 shoulder flexor de프레스or retractor 스트레칭
Seated shoulder press barbell
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on a bench with a backrest, feet flat on the floor. Hold a barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing forward), hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Rest the barbell on your upper chest.
2. Press the barbell overhead by extending your elbows, keeping your back firmly against the backrest.
3. Fully extend your arms at the top of the movement, ensuring the barbell is directly above your head.
4. Lower the barbell back down to your upper chest with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your wrists straight and aligned with your forearms to avoid strain.
2. Avoid locking your elbows at the top of the movement to protect your joints.
3. Exhale as you press the barbell up and inhale as you lower it down.

translations.name: Seated side stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
2. Extend your right arm overhead, keeping your left hand holding on the bench for support.
3. Lean your torso to the left, reaching your right arm over your head and to the left side, feeling the stretch along the right side of your body.
4. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then slowly return to the starting position. Repeat on the other side by extending your left arm overhead and leaning to the right.
translations.tips:
1. Move into the stretch gradually, ensuring that your movements are controlled to avoid muscle strain.
2. Ensure your supporting hand is firmly placed on the bench to maintain stability.
3. Perform the stretch on a stable, non-slip surface to ensure safety and reduce discomfort.

translations.name: Seated single leg hamstring stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you.
2. Bend your left knee and place the sole of your left foot against your right inner thigh. Keep your right leg extended and straight.
3. Reach forward with both hands towards your right foot, keeping your back straight. Aim to grasp your toes or reach as far as comfortable.
4. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in your right hamstring. Slowly release and return to the starting position. Repeat on the other side by extending your left leg and bending your right knee.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your extended leg straight and avoid locking your knee to protect the joint.
2. Ensure your bent knee is relaxed and the sole of your foot is comfortably placed against your inner thigh.
3. Move into the stretch gradually and with control to prevent muscle strain or injury.

translations.name: Seated straight leg calf stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you.
2. Lightly bend both knees and extend your right foot, reaching forward to grasp your toes with your right hand.
3. Gently pull your toes towards you, feeling the stretch in your calf muscle.
4. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then slowly release. Repeat on the left leg.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain a straight back as you reach forward to enhance the effectiveness of the stretch.
2. Engage your core muscles to support your lower back and avoid rounding your shoulders.
3. Move slowly and with control to prevent overstretching or injury.

translations.name: Seated v up
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you and your arms placed on the floor slightly behind you for support.
2. Engage your core and lift your torso off the floor, bringing your knees towards your chest to form a V shape with your body.
3. Hold the top position for a moment, squeezing your abdominal muscles.
4. Lower your legs and torso back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift into the V position and inhaling as you lower back down.
2. Avoid using momentum to lift your legs and torso; focus on using your abdominal muscles.
3. Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders to maintain proper form.

translations.name: Shadow boxing
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, one foot slightly in front of the other, and your knees slightly bent. Position your hands in front of your face in a guard position, with your elbows close to your body.
Begin throwing punches in the air, alternating between jabs, crosses, hooks, and uppercuts. Use a full range of motion and rotate your hips and shoulders to generate power.
Move around as you throw your punches, incorporating footwork to simulate a real boxing match. Step forward, backward, and side to side, keeping light on your feet.
Continue shadow boxing for the desired duration, maintaining a steady pace and controlled movements.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on proper form and technique with each punch to maximize effectiveness and prevent injury.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling with each punch and inhaling between movements.
3. Keep your hands up in the guard position to protect your face, even while moving and throwing punches.
4. Use your lower body to generate power, rotating your hips and pivoting on your feet with each punch.
5. Maintain a light and agile stance, allowing you to move quickly and fluidly.

translations.name: Shoulder abduction
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms at your sides.
2. Slowly raise your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, forming a T shape with your body. Keep your palms facing down and your elbows slightly bent.
3. Hold the position for a moment, feeling the activation in your shoulders.
4. Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Move your arms slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you raise your arms and inhaling as you lower them.
3. Ensure your shoulders stay relaxed and away from your ears throughout the movement.
4. Avoid using momentum to lift your arms; focus on using your shoulder muscles.

translations.name: Shoulder circle
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms relaxed, hanging straight down by your sides.
2. Begin by making small circular motions with your shoulders, moving them backward in a controlled manner.
3. Gradually increase the size of the circles, maintaining a steady pace.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Move your shoulders slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you complete each circle and inhaling as you start the next.
3. Avoid using momentum to move your shoulders; focus on using your shoulder muscles.

translations.name: Shoulder extension
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms straight by your sides.
2. Slowly extend your arms straight forward, and up overhead, keeping them straight and parallel to each other .
3. Hold the top position for a moment. Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you raise your arms overhead and inhaling as you lower them back down.
2. Ensure your shoulders stay relaxed and away from your ears throughout the movement.
3. Avoid using momentum to lift your arms; focus on using your shoulder muscles.

translations.name: Side lateral raise Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the middle of a resistance band under both feet. Hold one handle in each hand, arms down by your sides, and palms facing inward.
2. Lift your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, forming a "T" shape with your body. Focusing on lifting with your shoulders and keeping your palms facing down.
3. Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you lift your arms and inhale as you lower them back down.
2. Use a resistance band that provides enough tension to challenge your muscles without compromising form.
3. Avoid swinging your body or using momentum to lift the band.

translations.name: Side lunges bodyweight
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your hands clasped in front of you for balance.
2. Take a big step to the right with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position. Your right knee should be bent and your left leg should remain straight.
3. Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
4. Repeat on the other side by stepping out to the left with your left foot, lowering into a lunge, and then returning to the starting position.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your knee does not extend past your toes in the lunge position to protect your knee joint.
2. Maintain a straight back and avoid leaning forward excessively during the lunge.
3. Breathe steadily, inhaling as you step out and lower into the lunge, and exhaling as you push back to the starting position.

translations.name: Side lying floor stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your left side with your legs stacked on top of each other and your body in a straight line. Rest your head on your left arm.
2. Extend your right arm straight over your head.
3. Slowly lower your right hand towards your left hand to stretch your back.
4. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in your back and shoulders. Slowly return to the starting position and repeat on the other side by lying on your right side and following the same steps.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your extended arm stays straight to maximize the stretch in your back and shoulders.
2. Keep your core engaged to support your lower back during the stretch.
3. Perform the stretch on a comfortable, non-slip surface to ensure safety and reduce discomfort.

translations.name: Side lying leg lift left
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your right side with your legs extended straight and stacked on top of each other. Rest your head on your right arm and place your left hand on your hips.
2. Slowly lift your left leg straight up towards the ceiling, keeping it straight and in line with your body.
3. Hold the top position for a moment, feeling the contraction in your outer thigh and hip.
4. Slowly lower your leg back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left side and perform the exercise with your right leg.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your leg and inhaling as you lower it.
2. Ensure your leg stays straight and in line with your body throughout the movement.
3. Avoid using momentum to lift your leg; focus on using your hip and thigh muscles.

translations.name: side lying leg lift right
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your left side with your legs extended straight and stacked on top of each other. Rest your head on your left arm and place your right hand on your hips.
2. Slowly lift your right leg straight up towards the ceiling, keeping it straight and in line with your body.
3. Hold the top position for a moment, feeling the contraction in your outer thigh and hip.
4. Slowly lower your leg back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your right side and perform the exercise with your left leg.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your leg and inhaling as you lower it.
2. Ensure your leg stays straight and in line with your body throughout the movement.
3. Avoid using momentum to lift your leg; focus on using your hip and thigh muscles.

translations.name: Side plank
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your right side with your legs extended straight and stacked on top of each other. Prop yourself up on your right elbow, keeping your elbow directly under your shoulder.
2. Engage your core and lift your hips off the floor, forming a straight line from your head to your feet.
3. Hold the position for the desired duration, keeping your body straight and your core tight.
4. Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position with control. Repeat on the other side by lying on your left side and following the same steps.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your elbow is directly under your shoulder to support your upper body.
2. Focus on keeping your head in line with your spine to prevent neck strain.
3. Maintain a straight body line, avoiding any sagging or lifting of the hips.
4. Perform the exercise on a comfortable, non-slip surface to ensure safety and reduce discomfort.

translations.name: Side Plank with hip lift
translations.instructions:
1. Begin in a side plank position on your right side, with your legs extended straight and stacked on top of each other. Prop yourself up on your right elbow, keeping your elbow directly under your shoulder. Raise your left arm straight toward the ceiling.
2. Engage your core and lift your hips off the floor, forming a straight line from your head to your feet.
3. Once in the top position, perform two small controlled bounces with your hips, lifting them slightly higher each time.
4. Lower your hips back to the floor without touching it and repeat the steps for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other side and perform the exercise on your left side.
translations.tips:
1. Move your hips slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your hips and inhaling as you lower them.
3. Ensure your elbow is directly under your shoulder to support your upper body.

translations.name: Side push up
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your right side with your legs bent and stacked on top of each other. Place your right arm across your chest and your left hand on the floor in front of you for support.
2. Engage your core and press your left hand into the floor, lifting your upper body off the floor.
3. Hold the top position for a moment, feeling the contraction in your chest and triceps.
4. Slowly lower your upper body back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left side and perform the exercise with your right hand for support.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower back down.
2. Ensure your supporting hand is firmly placed on the floor to provide stability.
3. Avoid using momentum to lift your upper body; focus on using your chest and triceps muscles.

translations.name: Side to side punch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent. Hold your fists up in front of your face in a guard position.
2. Rotate your torso to the left as you punch across your body with your right hand, extending your arm fully. Keep your legs and hips stationary.
3. Quickly return to the starting position, then rotate your torso to the right as you punch across your body with your left hand.
4. Continue alternating punches from side to side for the desired duration or number of repetitions, maintaining a steady rhythm and controlled movements.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on rotating your torso to generate power in your punches while keeping your legs and hips stationary.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you punch and inhaling as you return to the starting position.
3. Keep your fists up in a guard position when not punching to simulate a defensive stance.

translations.name: Side to side push up
translations.instructions:
1. Begin in a standard push-up position with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
2. Lower your body into a push-up, bending both arms but leaning more to the right side, bringing your chest closer to your right hand.
3. Push through both hands to return to the starting position.
4. Lower your body into a push-up again, this time leaning more to the left side, bringing your chest closer to your left hand. Continue alternating sides with each push-up for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lower your chest and inhaling as you push back up.
2. Ensure your hands are placed firmly on the floor to provide stability during the exercise.
3. Focus on using your chest and triceps muscles to push back up to the starting position.

translations.name: Side wrist pull stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Extend your right arm across your torso at chest height, keeping it bent and your forearm parallel to the floor.
3. Bend your left arm and use your left hand to grasp your right hand, gently pulling your right wrist backward to feel a stretch in your right wrist and forearm.
4. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then slowly release. Repeat on the other side.
translations.tips:
1. Move your wrist slowly and with control to avoid any sudden movements that could cause strain.
2. Breathe deeply and steadily as you hold the stretch.
3. Focus on relaxing into the stretch to allow your muscles to lengthen fully.

translations.name: Single arm cable row left
translations.instructions:
1. Set the cable pulley to a mid position and attach a single handle. Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the handle with your left hand, arm extended towards the machine.
2. Slightly bend your knees, push your hips backward, and lean forward slightly for balance.
3. Pull the handle towards your torso, bending your elbow and squeezing your shoulder blade back. Keeping your elbow close to your body.
4. Slowly extend your arm back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch to the right arm.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you pull the handle towards your torso and inhale as you extend your arm back.
2. Use a weight that provides enough resistance to challenge your muscles without compromising form.
3. Avoid using your body to generate momentum; keep the movement isolated to your arm and back.

translations.name: Single arm cable row right
translations.instructions:
1. Set the cable pulley to a mid position and attach a single handle. Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the handle with your right hand, arm extended towards the machine.
2. Slightly bend your knees, push your hips backward, and lean forward slightly for balance.
3. Pull the handle towards your torso, bending your elbow and squeezing your shoulder blade back. Keeping your elbow close to your body.
4. Slowly extend your arm back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch to the right arm.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you pull the handle towards your torso and inhale as you extend your arm back.
2. Use a weight that provides enough resistance to challenge your muscles without compromising form.
3. Avoid using your body to generate momentum; keep the movement isolated to your arm and back.

translations.name: Single arm overhead extension Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and secure one end of the resistance band under your left foot. Hold the other end of the band with your right hand, raising your right arm overhead, keeping your elbow close to your head and your palm facing forward.
2. Start with your right elbow bent and your hand behind your head.
3. Extend your right arm upward, fully straightening your elbow. Then, slowly lower your hand back to the starting position with control. 
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the left arm.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you extend your arm upward and inhale as you lower it back down.
2. Avoid arching your back; keep your posture upright.
3. Focus on engaging your triceps throughout the movement.

translations.name: 싱글 암 overhead extension 저항 밴드
Single arm standing low cable row
translations.instructions:
1. Set the cable pulley to a low position and attach a single handle. Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the handle with your left hand, arm extended towards the machine.
2. Slightly bend your knees, push your hips backward, and lean forward slightly for balance.
3. Pull the handle towards your torso, bending your elbow and squeezing your shoulder blade back. Keeping your elbow close to your body.
4. Slowly extend your arm back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch to the right arm.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you pull the handle towards your torso and inhale as you extend your arm back.
2. Use a weight that provides enough resistance to challenge your muscles without compromising form.
3. Avoid using your body to generate momentum; keep the movement isolated to your arm and back.

translations.name: 싱글 암 스탠딩 low 케이블 로우
Single kettlebell deadlift
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand in front of your body. Keep your back straight and shoulders back.
2. Bend at your hips and knees to lower the kettlebell towards the floor, keeping your back flat.
3. Push through your heels to return to the standing position, extending your hips and knees.
4. Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement, then lower the kettlebell back down in a controlled manner. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other hand and repeat.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you lift the kettlebell and inhale as you lower it.
2. Ensure your knees do not cave inward; keep them aligned with your toes.
3. Focus on hinging at the hips rather than bending at the waist.

translations.name: Single leg calf raise leg press machine
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on the leg press machine and straighten your left leg, placing your forefoot on the platform, while your heel hangs off the edge. Your right foot should be resting on the floor.
2. Press through your forefoot to lift the platform upward by extending your ankle and squeezing your calf at the top of the movement.
3. Slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position with control, ensuring you achieve a full range of motion. 
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the right leg.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your knee slightly bent to reduce strain on your joints.
2. Ensure your heel drops below the edge of the platform to achieve a full range of motion.
3. Maintain a steady pace to ensure consistent tension on the muscles.

translations.name: 싱글 레그 calf raise 레그프레스 머신
Single leg incline 45 degree leg press machine
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on the leg press machine and place your left foot flat on the platform while your right foot rests on the floor.
2. Engage your core and keep your back pressed against the seat. Release the safety handles of the leg press machine.
3. Press through your left foot to extend your leg fully, pushing the platform upward.
4. Slowly lower the platform back down to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the right leg.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you press the platform upward and inhale as you lower it back down.
2. Ensure your knee stays aligned with your toes to avoid unnecessary strain.
3. Keep your knee slightly bent at the top of the movement to reduce strain on your joints.

translations.name: 싱글 레그 인클라인 45 도 레그프레스 머신
Single leg romanian deadlift dumbbell
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand. Shift your weight onto the right leg and keep it straight.
2. Hinge at your hips, extending your left leg straight back while lowering the dumbbell towards the floor.
3. Continue lowering until your upper body and extended leg are parallel to the floor and level from ankle to shoulders.
4. Return to the starting position by driving through your standing heel. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you lower the dumbbell and inhale as you return to the start.
2. Maintain a slight bend in the standing knee to avoid joint strain.
3. Practice the movement without weights to master the form before adding resistance.

translations.name: Single leg romanian deadlifts right cable Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Attach one end of the resistance band to a low anchor point and hold the other end in your right hand. Stand on your left leg with your right leg slightly bent and your left foot firmly on the floor.
2. Hinge at your hips and lower your torso until it's parallel to the floor while extending your right leg straight back, keeping your left knee slightly bent. 
3. Return to the starting position by driving your hips forward and bringing your right leg back down.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the left leg.
translations.tips:
1. Exhale as you hinge forward and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Maintain a slight bend in your standing knee for balance and stability.
3. Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders.

translations.name: 싱글 레그 루마니안 데드리프트s right 케이블 저항 밴드
Skipping
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet together and arms by your sides as if holding the handles of a skipping rope, one in each hand. Engage your core and maintain an upright posture.
2. Swing your hands in a circular motion as if turning a skipping rope, and jump lightly off the floor, keeping your knees slightly bent and landing softly on your forefeet.
3. Keep your jumps low and quick, simulating skipping with a rope. Repeat for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily and rhythmically to maintain oxygen flow during the exercise.
2. Focus on keeping your jumps low to the floor to minimize impact on your joints.
3. Stay relaxed and avoid tensing your shoulders and arms.

translations.name: Smith machine bulgarian split squat
translations.instructions:
1. Position yourself under the bar of a smith machine, resting it across your shoulders, and hold it firmly with both hands. Place your right foot back on a bench or box behind you, ensuring your left foot is firmly planted on the floor in front.
2. Lower your body by bending your left knee, keeping your torso upright and your right knee pointing towards the floor.
3. Continue lowering until your left thigh is parallel to the floor and your right knee is close to, but not touching the floor.
4. Push through your left heel to return to the starting position, extending your left leg fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your right leg.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your front knee stays in line with your toes to avoid unnecessary strain on the knee joint.
2. Keep your torso upright and avoid leaning forward or backward excessively.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.

translations.name: Smith machine calf raise
translations.instructions:
1. Position yourself under the bar of a smith machine, resting it across your shoulders, and hold it firmly with both hands. Place your feet shoulder-width apart with your toes pointing forward.
2. Lift your heels off the floor by pushing through your forefeet, raising your body upwards.
3. Continue lifting until you are standing on your forefeet, then slowly lower your heels back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your heels and inhaling as you lower them back down.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and reduce injury risk.
3. Ensure your knees remain slightly bent to avoid knee strain.

translations.name: Smith machine press decline
translations.instructions:
1. Position a decline bench under the smith machine. Adjust the bench so that the barbell is at chest height when lying on the bench.
2. Lie on the decline bench with your feet secured under the foot pads. Grip the barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
3. Unrack the barbell and lower it to your chest until it slightly touches it.
4. Press the barbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you press the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it down.
2. Ensure your feet are securely placed under the foot pads to prevent slipping.
3. Focus on using your chest muscles to press the weight, avoiding excessive strain on your shoulders.

translations.name: Smith machine press flat
translations.instructions:
1. Position a flat bench under the smith machine. Adjust the bench so that the barbell is at chest height when lying on the bench.
2. Lie on the flat bench with your feet flat on the floor. Grip the barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
3. Unrack the barbell and lower it to your chest until it slightly touches it.
4. Press the barbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you press the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it down.
2. Ensure your feet are firmly set on the floor for balance and support.
3. Focus on using your chest muscles to press the weight, avoiding excessive strain on your shoulders.

translations.name: Smith machine press incline
translations.instructions:
1. Position an incline bench under the smith machine. Adjust the bench so that the barbell is at chest height when lying on the bench.
2. Lie on the incline bench with your feet flat on the floor. Grip the barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
3. Unrack the barbell and lower it to your chest until it slightly touches it.
4. Press the barbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you press the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it down.
2. Ensure your feet are firmly set on the floor for balance and support.
3. Focus on using your chest muscles to press the weight, avoiding excessive strain on your shoulders.

translations.name: Smith machine shoulder press
translations.instructions:
1. Position an adjustable bench under the Smith machine and set it to a vertical position. Adjust the barbell to your shoulder height.
2. Sit on the bench with your back straight and feet flat on the floor. Grip the barbell with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
3. Unrack the barbell and press it upward until your arms are fully extended above your head.
4. Lower the barbell back to shoulder height with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your back remains flat against the bench throughout the movement.
2. Focus on using your shoulder muscles to press the weight, avoiding excessive strain on your neck.
3. Keep your elbows slightly bent at the top to avoid straining your elbow joints.

translations.name: Spider curl barbell
translations.instructions:
1. Set an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and lie face down, ensuring your chest is supported and your feet are firm on the floor. Hold a barbell with an underhand grip and hands shoulder-width apart.
2. Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
3. Curl the barbell towards your shoulders by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary, and focusing on contracting your biceps.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Use a full range of motion to fully engage the biceps.
2. Ensure your elbows remain stationary and do not flare out during the movement.
3. Adjust the weight of the barbell to provide enough challenge without compromising form.

translations.name: Spider curl dumbbells simultaneous
translations.instructions:
1. Set an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and lie face down, ensuring your chest is supported and your feet are firm on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
2. Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
3. Curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary, and focusing on contracting your biceps.
4. Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Use a full range of motion to fully engage the biceps.
2. Ensure your elbows remain stationary and do not flare out during the movement.
3. Adjust the weight of the dumbbells to provide enough challenge without compromising form.

translations.name: Spider curl ez bar
translations.instructions:
1. Set an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and lie face down, ensuring your chest is supported and your feet are firm on the floor. Hold an EZ bar with an underhand grip and hands shoulder-width apart.
2. Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
3. Curl the EZ bar towards your shoulders by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary, and focusing on contracting your biceps.
4. Slowly lower the EZ bar back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Use a full range of motion to fully engage the biceps.
2. Ensure your elbows remain stationary and do not flare out during the movement.
3. Adjust the weight of the barbell to provide enough challenge without compromising form.

translations.name: Spider plank
translations.instructions:
1. Begin in a standard plank position with your forearms on the floor, elbows directly under your shoulders, and your body forming a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core and keep your feet hip-width apart.
2. Lift your right foot off the floor and bring your right knee towards your right elbow, keeping your hips level and your core tight.
3. Return your right foot to the starting position, maintaining a stable plank position.
4. Repeat the movement with your left leg, bringing your left knee towards your left elbow. Continue alternating legs for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you bring your knee towards your elbow and inhaling as you return to the starting position.
2. Focus on keeping your hips level and avoiding excessive torso rotation.
3. Use a mat to cushion your forearms and feet for added comfort.

translations.name: Standing Ab Wheel Rollout
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding an ab wheel with both hands. Engage your core and maintain an upright posture.
2. Bend at your hips and knees to lower the ab wheel to the floor in front of your feet. Keep your arms extended and your back straight.
3. Slowly roll the ab wheel forward, extending your body into a plank position. Ensure your core remains tight and your back stays straight throughout the movement.
4. Roll the ab wheel back towards your feet, bending at your hips and knees to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and reduce injury risk.
2. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you roll out and inhaling as you return.
3. Keep your back straight and avoid sagging or arching your lower back.

translations.name: standing adductor stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward. Keep your hands to your hips.
2. Shift your weight to your right leg, bending your right knee while keeping your left leg straight. Lean your torso slightly forward.
3. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling a stretch along the inner thigh of your left leg.
4. Return to the starting position and repeat the stretch on the other side by shifting your weight to your left leg and bending your left knee.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your bent knee does not extend past your toes to avoid unnecessary strain on the knee joint.
2. Perform the stretch slowly and with control to maximize muscle flexibility and reduce injury risk.
3. Gradually increase the depth of the stretch as your flexibility improves.

translations.name: Standing arnold press dumbbell
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height. Your palms should be facing backward, and your elbows should be bent.
2. Begin to press the dumbbells overhead. As you press, rotate your palms so they face forward at the top of the movement.
3. Fully extend your arms at the top, ensuring your biceps are near your ears.
4. Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position by rotating your palms back towards your shoulders. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Rotate your wrists smoothly during the pressing and lowering phases to avoid strain.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you press the weights up and inhaling as you lower them back down.
3. Focus on the full range of motion to engage all parts of the shoulder muscles effectively.

translations.name: Standing bow hamstring stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet together and your arms by your sides. Engage your core and maintain an upright posture.
2. Hinge at your hips and lower your torso towards your legs, reaching your hands towards the back of your feet. Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders.
3. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling a stretch along the back of your legs. 
4. Return to the starting position and repeat the stretch as needed.
translations.tips:
1. Perform the stretch slowly and with control to maximize muscle flexibility and reduce injury risk.
2. Ensure your weight is distributed evenly on both feet to maintain balance.
3. Focus on keeping your back straight and avoiding excessive rounding of your spine.

translations.name: Standing cable oblique twist
translations.instructions:
1. Stand sideways to a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Attach a handle to a mid-pulley and grasp it with both hands. Bend your arms at 90 degrees and keep your elbows close to your torso sides.
2. Engage your core and keep your arms stationary as you twist your torso away from the machine, pulling the handle across your body.
3. Twist until your shoulders are turned 90 degrees from the machine, feeling the stretch in your oblique muscles.
4. Slowly return to the starting position with control, keeping your arms straight. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
translations.tips:
1. Use your oblique muscles to twist, not your arms, hips, or legs.
2. Keep your feet planted firmly on the floor.
3. Adjust the cable machine weight to a challenging but manageable level.

translations.name: Standing calf raise Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a resistance band under your forefeet. Hold the handles of the band by your sides.
2. Slowly lift your heels off the floor by pushing through your forefeet, raising your body upwards.
3. Continue lifting until you are standing on your forefeet, feeling a strong contraction in your calves.
4. Slowly lower your heels back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on pushing through your forefeet, avoiding excessive pressure on your toes.
2. Move slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and avoid injury.
3. Ensure the resistance band is secure and provides enough challenge without compromising form.

translations.name: Standing calf raises smith machine
translations.instructions:
1. Stand in front of the Smith machine bar and place both hands on the bar keeping your arms straight. Place your forefeet shoulder-width apart on a low platform, heels extended to the floor.
2. Slowly lift your heels off the floor by pushing through your forefeet, raising your body upwards.
3. Continue lifting until you are standing on your forefeet, feeling a strong contraction in your calves.
4. Slowly lower your heels back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on pushing through your forefeet, avoiding excessive pressure on your toes.
2. Move slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and avoid injury.
3. Keep your knees slightly bent to avoid strain or injury of your joints.

translations.name: Standing dumbbell alternate shoulder press
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing forward and elbows pointing to the floor.
2. Press the right dumbbell overhead until your arm is fully extended, keeping the left dumbbell at shoulder height.
3. Lower the right dumbbell back to shoulder height with control and then press the left dumbbell overhead until your arm is fully extended.
4. Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on your breathing: exhale while pressing the dumbbell up and inhale while lowering it.
2. Avoid shrugging your shoulders to minimize neck strain.
3. Incorporate shoulder warm-up exercises to prepare the muscles for the workout.

translations.name: Standing front raise dumbbell
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms fully extended in front of your hips and palms facing your thighs.
2. Raise both arms straight in front of you until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
3. Lower your arms back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Warm up your shoulders with light exercises before starting to prevent injury.
2. Keep your head and neck in a neutral position to avoid unnecessary strain.
3. Focus on your breathing: exhale as you lift the dumbbell and inhale as you lower it.

translations.name: Standing gastrocnemius stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand facing a bench with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Place your right foot on the bench, keeping your heel on the bench and your toes pointing up. Your left foot should remain flat on the floor.
3. Lean forward slightly, grab your right heel with your right hand, and straighten your leg while lightly pulling your forefoot.
4. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch legs and repeat. Perform the stretch for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your knees slightly bent to avoid strain or injury of your joints.
2. Gradually increase the depth of the stretch as your flexibility improves.
3. Use a bench at a comfortable height to ensure an effective stretch.

translations.name: Standing hamstring calf stretch with Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a resistance band in both hands. Place the center of the band under your right forefoot.
2. Extend your right leg straight out in front of you, keeping your heel on the floor and your toes pointing upward. Hold the ends of the resistance band to create tension.
3. Gently pull on the resistance band, leaning your torso forward to increase the stretch in your right hamstring. Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders.
4. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch to your left leg and repeat. Perform the stretch for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on keeping your extended leg straight and your toes pointing upward.
2. Ensure your standing leg remains slightly bent to maintain balance.
3. Use a resistance band with appropriate tension to provide an effective stretch.

translations.name: 스탠딩 hamstring calf 스트레칭 with 저항 밴드
Standing lat cable pull over
translations.instructions:
1. Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and hold it with both hands, arms extended and palms facing down.
2. Slightly bend your knees, hinge at your hips, and lean forward slightly. Keep your arms straight and pull the bar down in an arc motion towards your thighs. 
3. Keep pulling the bar down until it reaches your thighs, feeling a strong contraction in your lats.
4. Slowly return the bar to the starting position with control, maintaining tension in your lats. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you pull the bar down and inhale as you return it to the starting position.
2. Focus on using your lat muscles to pull the bar, avoiding excessive movement of your arms.
3. Keep your shoulders relaxed and avoid shrugging them towards your ears.

translations.name: Standing lateral stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, arms relaxed by your sides. Engage your core and maintain an upright posture.
2. Place your right hand behind your head. Keep your left hand extended down the side of your leg.
3. Gently bend your torso to the left as far as possible and hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling a stretch along the right side of your torso.
4. Return to the starting position and repeat the stretch on the other side.
translations.tips:
1. Perform the stretch slowly and with control to maximize muscle flexibility and reduce injury risk.
2. Gradually increase the depth of the stretch as your flexibility improves.
3. Focus on keeping your back straight and avoiding excessive rounding of your spine.

translations.name: Standing leg elevated hamstring stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand facing an incline bench with your feet shoulder-width apart. Place your right heel on the edge of the bench headrest, keeping your leg straight and toes pointing upward.
2. Engage your core and hinge at your hips, leaning your torso towards your right leg.
3. Reach towards your right foot with both hands, feeling a stretch along the back of your right leg (hamstring). Hold this position for 20-30 seconds.
4. Return to the starting position and switch legs. Perform the stretch for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on keeping your extended leg straight and your toes pointing upward.
2. Ensure your standing leg remains slightly bent to maintain balance.
3. Set the bench at an elevated surface at a comfortable height to ensure an effective stretch.

translations.name: Standing oblique crunch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, placing your hands behind your head with your elbows flared out to the sides. Engage your core and maintain an upright posture.
2. Lift your right knee towards your right elbow while simultaneously bending your torso to the right to bring your elbow and knee closer together.
3. Return to the starting position by lowering your right leg and straightening your torso.
4. Repeat the movement on the left side, lifting your left knee towards your left elbow. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you crunch to the side and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Ensure your standing leg remains slightly bent to maintain balance.
3. Keep your back straight and avoid leaning forward or backward during the exercise.

translations.name: Standing one arm chest stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your right side facing near a wall with your feet shoulder-width apart. Extend your right arm to the side at shoulder height and place your palm flat against the wall.
2. Slowly turn your body away from the wall, keeping your arm extended and feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulder.
3. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, maintaining a gentle pressure.
4. Return to the starting position and repeat the stretch on the left side. Perform the stretch for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on keeping your extended arm straight and your palm flat against the wall.
2. Ensure your shoulders remain relaxed and avoid tensing them up towards your ears.
3. Use a gentle motion to avoid over-stretching and potential muscle strain.

translations.name: Standing overhand shoulder stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, arms extended straight out in front of you at shoulder level, palms facing down.
2. Swiftly raise your right arm overhead while simultaneously lowering your left arm down towards your thigh to your side. Keeping both arms straight.
3. Swiftly return to the starting position and repeat the stretch by raising your left arm overhead and lowering your right arm. 
4. Perform the stretch for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily throughout the stretch, exhaling as you raise and lower your arms and inhaling as you return to the starting position.
2. Focus on keeping both arms straight and avoiding any twisting or turning of your torso.
3. Use a gentle motion to avoid over-stretching and potential muscle strain.

translations.name: Standing quadriceps stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and find a stable surface to hold onto for balance if needed.
2. Bend your right knee and bring your heel towards your glutes. Grasp your right ankle with your right hand.
3. Keep your knees close together and lightly pull your right ankle upward, feeling a stretch along the front of your right thigh.
4. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release your right ankle and return to the starting position. Repeat the stretch on the left leg.
translations.tips:
1. Perform the stretch slowly and with control to maximize muscle flexibility and reduce injury risk.
2. Ensure your supporting leg remains slightly bent to maintain balance.
3. Focus on keeping your torso upright and avoid leaning forward or backward.

translations.name: Standing quadriceps stretch side angle
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and find a stable surface to hold onto for balance if needed.
2. Bend your right knee and bring your heel towards your glutes. Grasp your right ankle with your right hand.
3. Keep your knees close together and lightly pull your right ankle upward, feeling a stretch along the front of your right thigh.
4. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release your right ankle and return to the starting position. Repeat the stretch on the left leg.
translations.tips:
1. Perform the stretch slowly and with control to maximize muscle flexibility and reduce injury risk.
2. Ensure your supporting leg remains slightly bent to maintain balance.
3. Focus on keeping your torso upright and avoid leaning forward or backward.

translations.name: Standing reverse shoulder stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your posture upright. Clasp your hands behind your back, interlocking your fingers.
2. Straighten your arms and slowly lift your hands upwards, feeling a stretch in your shoulders.
3. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, and return to the starting position by lowering your hands and releasing the clasp. 
4. Repeat the stretch for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on keeping your arms straight and avoiding any bending at the elbows.
2. Ensure your shoulders remain relaxed and avoid tensing them up towards your ears.
3. Use a gentle motion to avoid over-stretching and potential muscle strain.

translations.name: Standing shoulder press dumbbell
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing forward and elbows pointing to the floor.
2. Press both dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended, keeping your elbows slightly bent at the top.
3. Slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control, maintaining tension in your shoulders.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you press the dumbbells up and inhale as you lower them down.
2. Focus on using your shoulder muscles to lift the weight, avoiding excessive strain on your neck.
3. Keep your back straight and avoid arching your lower back during the press.

translations.name: Standing side leg raises
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands on your hips for balance.
2. Shift your weight onto your left leg and slowly lift your right leg out to the side as high as you comfortably can, keeping it straight.
3. Hold the raised leg for a second, and slowly lower your right leg back to the starting position with control. 
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left leg.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on keeping your torso upright and avoid leaning to the side.
2. Keep your supporting leg slightly bent to maintain balance.
3. Start with a smaller range of motion and gradually increase it as your strength improves.

translations.name: Standing side slope stretch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your posture upright. Raise your right arm overhead and allow your left hand to hang by your side.
2. Gently lean your torso to the left, reaching your right arm over your head and feeling a stretch along the right side of your torso.
3. Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, and slowly return to the starting position.
4. Perform the stretch for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your shoulders remain relaxed and avoid tensing them up towards your ears.
2. Use a gentle motion to avoid over-stretching and potential muscle strain.
3. Gradually increase the depth of the stretch as your flexibility improves.

translations.name: Standing swimmer
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, your left arm extended up, your right arm extended down, and your palms facing forward.
2. Simultaneously move your right arm up while moving your left arm down and lightly rotating your core, mimicking a swimming motion. Keep your arms straight throughout the movement.
3. Continue alternating arms in a smooth, controlled motion for the desired repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Keep your core tight to maintain balance and stability.
2. Focus on keeping your movements smooth and fluid, avoiding any jerky motions.
3. Breathe steadily: exhale as you move your arms back and inhale as you return to the starting position.

translations.name: Standing toe touch
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your posture upright and your arms relaxed by your sides.
2. Simultaneously lift your right leg straight up to hip height while raising your left arm to touch your right toes.
3. Return your right leg and left arm to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the movement on the opposite side. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on keeping both your leg and arm straight throughout the movement.
2. Ensure your supporting leg remains slightly bent to maintain balance.
3. Keep your core tight to maintain balance and stability.

translations.name: Standing Y raise
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, with your arms extended forward, parallel to each other.
2. With slightly bent elbows, raise both arms diagonally overhead to form a "Y" shape.
3. Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position. 
4. Repeat the movement for the desired repetitions
translations.tips:
1. Move slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and avoid injury.
2. Breathe steadily: exhale as you raise your arms and inhale as you lower them.
3. Focus on keeping your arms in a diagonal line to form a "Y" shape and avoid any excessive swinging.

translations.name: Star crunches
translations.instructions:
1. Lie flat on your back with your arms and legs extended out to form an "X" shape. Keep your core engaged and your lower back pressed into the floor.
2. Crunch your abdominal muscles to lift your shoulders and legs off the floor and lift your arms and legs towards each other, reaching your hands towards your ankles.
3. Lower your arms and legs back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you lift your arm and leg, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Focus on using your abdominal muscles to lift your arms and legs off the floor.
3. Use a mat to provide cushioning and support for your back.

translations.name: Star jump
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet together and your arms by your sides, maintaining an upright posture.
2. Bend your knees slightly and lower into a half-squat position to prepare for the jump.
3. Explosively jump upwards, spreading your legs out to the sides and extending your arms overhead to form a star shape in mid-air.
4. Land softly with your feet together and your arms by your sides, immediately bending your knees to absorb the impact. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on using your leg muscles to generate the jump, avoiding excessive strain on your knees.
2. Ensure your knees are slightly bent as you land to absorb the impact and prevent injury.
3. Start with smaller jumps to perfect your form before increasing the height.

translations.name: Stationary Exercise Bike
translations.instructions:
1. Adjust the seat height so that when you sit on the bike, your feet can comfortably reach the pedals with a slight bend in your knees at the bottom of the pedal stroke.
2. Sit on the bike with your back straight, and grip the handlebars lightly. Place your feet securely on the pedals, ensuring they are aligned properly.
3. Begin pedaling by pushing down on one pedal while pulling up on the opposite pedal, creating a smooth, circular motion.
4. Adjust the resistance level using the bike's control knob or digital settings to match your desired intensity. Continue pedaling for the duration of your workout.
translations.tips:
1. Maintain good posture by keeping your back straight and shoulders relaxed to avoid strain.
2. Start with a lower resistance level if you are a beginner and gradually increase it as you build strength and endurance.
3. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, inhaling deeply and exhaling fully.

translations.name: Step up on chair bodyweight
translations.instructions:
1. Stand facing a sturdy chair with your feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your posture upright.
2. Place your left foot firmly on the chair, ensuring your entire foot is supported.
3. Push through your left heel to lift your body onto the chair, bringing your right foot up to stand on the chair with both feet.
4. Step down carefully with your right foot first, followed by your left foot. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you step up and inhale as you step down.
2. Focus on pushing through your heel to engage your glutes and quadriceps.
3. Ensure the chair or bench is stable and secure before starting the exercise.

translations.name: Straight bar cable pull down
translations.instructions:
1. Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar attached to the high pulley. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, palms facing down.
2. Slightly bend your knees, push your hips back, and lean your torso forward slightly. Pull the bar down towards your thighs in a straight line, keeping your arms straight and your elbows slightly bent.
3. Pull the bar down until it reaches your thighs, feeling a strong contraction in your lats.
4. Slowly return the bar to the starting position with control, maintaining tension in your lats. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you pull the bar down and inhale as you return it to the starting position.
2. Adjust the weight on the cable machine to provide enough challenge without compromising form.
3. Keep your shoulders relaxed and avoid shrugging them towards your ears.

translations.name: Straight bar cable row close grip
translations.instructions:
1. Sit at a cable row machine with your feet placed on the footrests and knees slightly bent. Grasp the straight bar with a close grip, palms facing down.
2. Engage your core and maintain a straight back. Pull the bar towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping your elbows close to your body.
3. Hold the contraction for a moment, feeling the muscles in your back activating.
4. Slowly extend your arms to return the bar to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you pull the bar towards you and inhale as you extend your arms.
2. Adjust the weight on the cable machine to provide enough challenge without compromising form.
3. Keep your shoulders relaxed and avoid shrugging them towards your ears.

translations.name: straight bar 케이블 로우 close grip
Straight bar cable row normal grip
translations.instructions:
1. Sit at a cable row machine with your feet placed on the footrests and knees slightly bent. Grasp the straight bar with hands just wider than shoulder-width, palms facing down.
2. Engage your core and maintain a straight back. Pull the bar towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping your elbows close to your body.
3. Hold the contraction for a moment, feeling the muscles in your back activating.
4. Slowly extend your arms to return the bar to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you pull the bar towards you and inhale as you extend your arms.
2. Adjust the weight on the cable machine to provide enough challenge without compromising form.
3. Keep your shoulders relaxed and avoid shrugging them towards your ears.

translations.name: straight bar 케이블 로우 normal grip
Sumo squat in between legs Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward. Place a resistance band under your feet.
2. Hold the ends of the resistance band with both hands in between your legs, keeping your hands at hip level.
3. Lower your body into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back. Keep your chest up and your back straight.
4. Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor, then push through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: inhale as you lower into the squat and exhale as you push back up to the starting position.
2. Focus on keeping your knees in line with your toes to avoid inward collapse.
3. Ensure your chest stays up and avoid leaning forward excessively.

translations.name: sumo 스쿼트 in between legs 저항 밴드
Sumo squat over shoulder Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward. Place a resistance band under your feet.
2. Hold the ends of the resistance band with both hands near your shoulders, palms facing forward and elbows pointing down.
3. Lower your body into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back. Keep your chest up and your back straight.
4. Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor, then push through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: inhale as you lower into the squat and exhale as you push back up to the starting position.
2. Focus on keeping your knees in line with your toes to avoid inward collapse.
3. Ensure your chest stays up and avoid leaning forward excessively.

translations.name: sumo 스쿼트 over shoulder 저항 밴드
Superman
translations.instructions:
1. Lie face down on the floor with your arms extended straight out in front of you and your legs extended straight behind you.
2. Simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor as high as you comfortably can, engaging your lower back and glutes.
3. Hold the lifted position for a second, feeling the contraction in your lower back and glutes.
4. Slowly lower your arms, chest, and legs back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on using your lower back and glute muscles to lift your body off the floor.
2. Keep your neck in a neutral position to avoid straining it.
3. Use a mat to provide cushioning and support for your body.

translations.name: Superman holds
translations.instructions:
1. Lie face down on the floor with your arms extended straight out in front of you and your legs extended straight behind you.
2. Simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor as high as you comfortably can, engaging your lower back and glutes.
3. Hold the lifted position for 5-10 seconds, feeling the contraction in your lower back and glutes.
4. Slowly lower your arms, chest, and legs back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on using your lower back and glute muscles to lift your body off the floor.
2. Keep your neck in a neutral position to avoid straining it.
3. Use a mat to provide cushioning and support for your body.

translations.name: Superman pulls
translations.instructions:
1. Lie face down on the floor with a resistance band secured to a low anchor point in front of you. Hold one end of the resistance band in each hand with your arms extended straight out in front of you and your legs extended straight behind you.
2. Simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor as high as you comfortably can, engaging your lower back and glutes.
3. While holding the lifted position, bend your elbows and pull the resistance band towards your shoulders, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
4. Extend your arms back out in front of you, keeping tension on the resistance band, and then slowly lower your arms, chest, and legs back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on using your lower back and glute muscles to lift your body off the floor.
2. Keep your neck in a neutral position to avoid straining it.
3. Use a mat to provide cushioning and support for your body.

translations.name: Superman pulls Resistance band
translations.instructions:
1. Lie face down on the floor with your arms extended straight out in front of you and your legs extended straight behind you.
2. Simultaneously lift your arms and chest off the floor as high as you comfortably can, engaging your lower back and glutes.
3. While holding the lifted position, bend your elbows and pull your arms back towards your shoulders, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
4. Extend your arms back out in front of you and slowly lower your chest back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you lift and pull the resistance band, and inhale as you extend your arms and lower your body.
2. Gradually increase the duration of the hold and the number of repetitions as your strength improves.
3. Keep your neck in a neutral position to avoid straining it.

translations.name: Supine push up
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Position your elbows to your sides with your forearms perpendicular to the floor and hands kept closed.
2. Engage your core and press your elbows into the floor, lifting your upper back and shoulders off the floor.
3. Hold the lifted position for a second, feeling the contraction in your upper back.
4. Slowly lower your upper back and shoulders back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you lift your upper back and shoulders, and inhale as you lower them back down.
2. Focus on using your shoulders and upper back muscles to lift your upper body off the floor.
3. Use a mat to provide cushioning and support for your back.

translations.name: Svend press decline bench
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured under the foot pads and your back firmly against the bench. Hold a weight plate with both hands, pressing your palms against the flat sides of the plate at chest level with your fingers pointing up.
2. Engage your chest and press the weight plate straight out in front of you by extending your arms.
3. Squeeze your chest muscles as you press the plate outward until your arms are fully extended.
4. Slowly bring the plate back to your chest with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on pressing through your chest, not your shoulders, to emphasize chest muscle activation.
2. Choose a weight that challenges you but allows you to maintain proper form.
3. Avoid locking your elbows completely at the top of the movement to keep tension on the chest muscles.

translations.name: Svend press flat bench
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor and your back firmly against the bench. Hold a weight plate with both hands, pressing your palms against the flat sides of the plate at chest level with your fingers pointing up.
2. Engage your chest and press the weight plate straight out in front of you by extending your arms.
3. Squeeze your chest muscles as you press the plate outward until your arms are fully extended.
4. Slowly bring the plate back to your chest with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on pressing through your chest, not your shoulders, to emphasize chest muscle activation.
2. Choose a weight that challenges you but allows you to maintain proper form.
3. Avoid locking your elbows completely at the top of the movement to keep tension on the chest muscles.

translations.name: Svend press incline bench
translations.instructions:
1. Lie on an incline bench with your feet flat on the floor and your back firmly against the bench. Hold a weight plate with both hands, pressing your palms against the flat sides of the plate at chest level with your fingers pointing up.
2. Engage your chest and press the weight plate straight out in front of you by extending your arms.
3. Squeeze your chest muscles as you press the plate outward until your arms are fully extended.
4. Slowly bring the plate back to your chest with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on pressing through your chest, not your shoulders, to emphasize chest muscle activation.
2. Choose a weight that challenges you but allows you to maintain proper form.
3. Avoid locking your elbows completely at the top of the movement to keep tension on the chest muscles.

translations.name: Trap bar deadlift
translations.instructions:
1. Stand in the center of a trap bar with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees and hips to lower your body, and grasp the trap bar handles with a neutral grip (palms facing in).
2. Engage your core; keep your chest up and your back straight. Push through your heels to lift the trap bar off the floor, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
3. Stand up straight, pulling your shoulders back at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower the bar back to the floor by bending your hips and knees, maintaining a straight back. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Ensure your chest remains up and your back stays straight to avoid injury.
2. Push through your heels to engage your glutes and hamstrings effectively.
3. Avoid locking your knees completely at the top of the movement to maintain tension in your muscles.

translations.name: Trap bar shrug
translations.instructions:
1. Stand in the center of a trap bar with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees and hips to lower your body, and grasp the handles of the trap bar with a neutral grip (palms facing in).
2. Stand up straight with the trap bar at arm's length by your sides, keeping your core engaged and back straight.
3. Lift your shoulders towards your ears, squeezing your traps at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you shrug your shoulders and inhale as you lower them.
2. Keep your neck relaxed and avoid straining it during the movement.
3. Ensure your back remains straight and avoid leaning forward or backward.

translations.name: Tricep cable kickback on crossover machine
translations.instructions:
1. Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, and set the cable at a low position. Hold the cable end in one hand and bend slightly forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
2. Position your upper arm parallel to the floor with your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle, keeping your elbow close to your side.
3. Extend your arm backward by straightening your elbow, keeping your upper arm stationary, and only moving your forearm.
4. Slowly return to the starting position by bending your elbow. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.
translations.tips:
1. Focus on using your triceps to perform the extension, avoiding excessive use of your shoulders or back.
2. Keep your upper arm stationary throughout the exercise to ensure proper form.
3. Use a weight that allows you to maintain control and proper form throughout the exercise.

translations.name: 트라이셉 케이블 kickback on crossover 머신
Tricep extension machine
translations.instructions:
1. Sit on the tricep extension machine, adjusting the seat height so that your upper arms comfortably rest on the arm pad, with your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
2. Grasp the handles with a neutral grip, ensuring your elbows are below the pivot point.
3. Push the handles forward by straightening your elbows and fully extending your arms while keeping your upper arms stationary.
4. Slowly return to the starting position by bending your elbows. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
translations.tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you extend your arms and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Focus on using your triceps to push the handles, avoiding excessive use of your shoulders or back.
